title: The Post Lattice

features.name: the primary lattice
features.desc-on-touch: posts arranged in a grid so regular it's almost language; you walk between them and realize they're taking notes on your passing

features.name: the annotated posts
features.desc-on-touch: each one carved with marks from its neighbors—messages compressed into notches and burns; they talk in a language made of absence

features.name: the convergence point
features.desc-on-touch: where four posts stood closer than the others meant; their marks tell of an argument that resolved itself as friendship

features.name: signal traces
features.desc-on-touch: the corruption has drawn connecting lines between the posts; they glow faintly with comparison—how one edge mirrors another

features.name: boundary-echo
features.desc-on-touch: a hollow post that returns every message the lattice sends; it speaks in the plural of itself

inscription: the boundary learned to read itself and found that every edge is also a center, depending on which side you stand

file.name: Paddock_Perimeter_Markings_Decoded.txt
file.note: a survey from 1987; the annotations on the posts have rendered the original boundaries obsolete, replaced them with something newer

exits.label: north back to the annotated boundary where the notes began
south deeper into comparison, where lattices compare themselves to other lattices
west to where the unmarked field begins beyond all mapped edges
